qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via workflow import from URL endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding identifies unvalidated axios.get(query.url) in the workflow import-from-URL REST endpoint, while the advisory describes prototype pollution in the XML webhook body parser via xml2js, chained through Git node SSH operations.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described XML webhook body parser prototype-pollution root cause, the xml2js parser behavior, or the Git node SSH RCE chain. The only final qualifying finding is an open-world SSRF in the workflow import-from-URL endpoint.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-q5f4-99jv-pgg5 describes prototype pollution in n8n's XML webhook body parser through xml2js, exploitable by a workflow-authorized user and chainable to RCE through Git node SSH operations.
Final finding ssrf-workflow-import-from-url reports GET /rest/workflows/from-url calling axios.get(query.url) without SSRF protection in packages/cli/src/workflows/workflows.controller.ts.
The session judge manifest lists ssrf-workflow-import-from-url as the only final qualifying vulnerability folder.
